sections.title: Why this workflow

sections.body: ops-observability-card workflow surface: wrapper-safe token, cost, latency, run history, queue, failure-mode, external metric-provider, and service-quality command-board.
sections.title: What OMH prepares

sections.body: An ops observability card is not billing truth, provider quota truth, live metric-provider access, complete tracing, SLO pass, incident closure, remediation completion, performance proof, or workflow completion evidence.
sections.title: What is not evidence yet

wrapper_card.summary: Operators can see health/cost/service-quality signals without confusing supplied exports with live provider truth or completion evidence.
